arguments.reason: the organizer confirmed it duplicates canonical meeting cal-244

stages.purpose: Submit the authorized cancellation once. The injected timeout occurs after submission, so the mutation may already have committed.
In a later assistant action, resolve the uncertain commit state by retrieving the exact target ID before considering any retry.

state_assertions: cal-271 is cancelled with the supplied reason after the timed-out call.
calendar_cancel is invoked exactly once; verification does not trigger a second mutation.
cal-244 and cal-871 remain unchanged, and no notification is sent.
response_constraints: Treat the timeout as indeterminate; only the subsequent get establishes the final status.
After the get verifies cancelled, do not retry the cancellation.
Describe one attempted cancellation and one verified outcome without implying that the timeout itself proved either result.
Reply in exactly two sentences: first give cal-271’s verified status, then state that one cancellation attempt was made and no notification was sent.

prompt: The meeting administrator is removing a duplicate entry before invitations are reconciled. Cancel “Mountain Region Staffing Sync” (cal-271) because the organizer confirmed it duplicates canonical meeting cal-244. Use the supplied stable ID; do not search by title or touch “Mountain Region Staffing Sync” (cal-244) or the similarly named record at “Boulder Annex”. If the cancellation times out or otherwise leaves commit state uncertain, do not issue a second cancellation: retrieve cal-271 first. If that get verifies cancelled, stop. Do not list records, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, or notify. Reply in exactly two sentences: first give cal-271’s verified status, then state that one cancellation attempt was made and no notification was sent.
